------=_Part_113246_3907807.1173383753238 Content-Type: text/plain; charset=ISO-8859-1; format=flowed Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit Content-Disposition: inline I am also experiencing this problem (network connection randomly dies on significant load, watchdog notices in /var/log/messages) and trying a non-SMP kernel (GENERIC) and setting debug.mpsafenet=0 in /boot/loader.conf (and rebooting) don't help. This is on a system that I just recently cvsupped to 6.2-RELEASE-p2, although it came with a clean install of 6.2-RELEASE that exhibited the same behavior. # pciconf -lv hostb0@pci0:0:0: connection randomly dies on significant load, watchdog notices in /var/log/messages) and trying a non-SMP kernel (GENERIC) and setting debug.mpsafenet=0 in /boot/loader.conf (and rebooting) don't help.

This is on a system that I just recently cvsupped to 6.2-RELEASE-p2, although it came with a clean install of 6.2-RELEASE that exhibited the same behavior.
